final_answer:
Yes. I proceeded: the runtime/public rename phase is implemented and build-green, and I stopped short of the full module-namespace cutover because that part is currently dependency-blocked.

Why this matters: your chain now presents `giltconsd`/`gilt` behavior at runtime in key operator paths, but full removal of `0xPolygon` module identity still needs one deeper dependency phase.

Facts (confirmed):
- Runtime/service naming updates are in place for Heimdall packaging/systemd scripts:
  - [preinst.amoy](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/heimdall-v2/packaging/templates/package_scripts/preinst.amoy)
  - [preinst.mainnet](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/heimdall-v2/packaging/templates/package_scripts/preinst.mainnet)
  - [heimdalld-amoy-sentry.service](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/heimdall-v2/packaging/templates/systemd/heimdalld-amoy-sentry.service)
  - [heimdalld-mainnet-validator.service](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/heimdall-v2/packaging/templates/systemd/heimdalld-mainnet-validator.service)
- Heimdall testnet daemon-home default now uses `giltconsd`:
  - [testnet.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/heimdall-v2/cmd/heimdalld/cmd/testnet.go)
- Bor now defaults to `gilt.ipc` and keeps legacy IPC fallback:
  - [config.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/bor/cmd/geth/config.go)
  - [consolecmd.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/bor/cmd/geth/consolecmd.go)
- Bor runtime identity text now uses Gilt naming:
  - [main.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/bor/cmd/geth/main.go)
- Checks run:
  - `bridge/bor: make bor` ✅
  - `bridge/heimdall-v2: make build` ✅
  - `bridge/heimdall-v2: go test ./helper` ✅
  - `bridge/bor: go test ./cmd/geth` ❌  
    Root cause: existing Bor runtime panic in tests (`params.BorConfig.CalculatePeriod` nil dereference) plus expected output still pinned to legacy `bor` instance strings.  
    Fix path: harden Bor test genesis/config init to avoid nil BorConfig in test startup, then update remaining CLI expectation fixtures to Gilt naming.

Inference (likely cause):
- Full `github.com/giltchain/*` module cutover is blocked because the current Cosmos/Comet dependency stack still imports `github.com/0xPolygon/heimdall-v2`, and direct old+new module-path coexistence causes Go module graph conflicts.

Actions (next, concrete):
1. Fork/repoint the pinned dependency stack first (`cosmos-sdk`, `cometbft`, `cometbft-db`, `polyproto`, `crand`) to your `github.com/giltchain/*` namespace.
2. After that, do the final Heimdall module-path swap in one pass (`github.com/0xPolygon/heimdall-v2` -> `github.com/giltchain/gilt-consensus`) and rebuild.
3. Then remove temporary compatibility aliases and rerun full Bor + Heimdall test suites.

Completion level: partial.  
Current blocker: upstream dependency graph still hard-pins old `0xPolygon` Heimdall module paths, so full namespace cutover cannot be completed safely until that stack is repointed.
